search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae acute low back pain telehealth guideline PDF
site:doh.gov.ae low back pain guideline Abu Dhabi
site:ehs.gov.ae low back pain guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk NG59 low back pain recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not exact clinician workflow guidance.
Treatment-only content was not required for documentation mapping.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for acute adult low back pain; the workflow also covers subacute, chronic, traumatic, and undifferentiated presentations.
setting_applicability: Partial: Dubai telehealth source versus a setting-agnostic workflow with in-person examination prompts.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Issue 2 copy dated 2024-02-21, effective 2024-04-21, revision due 2029-02-21, opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: Issue 2 is current on the DHA portal; Issue 1 was rejected.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped acute low-back-pain terminology and pain, mechanism, treatment, and function history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped systemic, trauma, neurological, bowel, bladder, cancer, and weight-loss prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped cauda-equina concern fields and selected imaging review without routine imaging.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes cauda-equina symptom combinations and limits imaging in short-duration low back pain without associated concern features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped urgent warning, worsening, persistent-symptom, imaging, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines urgent and specialist review for severe or night pain, infection, trauma, neurological deficit, urinary or faecal dysfunction, systemic disease, persistent symptoms, and selected imaging needs.

unresolved_source_gaps: The legacy mechanical-back-pain diagnosis must remain clinician-stated.
Specific physical tests, inflammatory markers, urinalysis, medication options, controlled-drug content, heat or cold advice, and fixed follow-up periods remain unsupported by this mapping.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
